concerns.text: T013 hardcodes 'EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset' (eegmmidb) as the data source. FR-001 requires the system to identify a dataset containing *paired* resting-state EEG and subjective fatigue ratings. The EegMMIDB dataset lacks these fatigue ratings, making T013 impossible to satisfy FR-001 without changing the dataset or the task logic. No task exists to identify/validate a correct dataset with fatigue ratings.

T027 mandates halting with an error if paired pre/post data is missing. However, plan.md Summary explicitly defines a conditional fallback to 'cross-sectional analysis' if only single-timepoint data exists. There is no task to implement this fallback logic, creating a gap where the implementation will fail the plan's architectural approach.

SC-001 requires a sample size N ≥ 30 participants. None of the tasks (including T013 for download or T015 for filtering) include a step to validate the final dataset size or re-download/switch sources if the chosen dataset yields N < 30. The project risks completing all tasks and failing the primary success criterion due to insufficient data volume.

Tasks T023, T024, and T019 implement 'topological attractor reconstruction' and 'adaptive simplification vs. degenerative noise' classification. These are not mentioned in the spec's FRs (FR-003/FR-004) or SCs. These are stale tags/scope creep from an external review comment not formalized into the spec, creating unrequested work.
